evidences.title: Giannis leads Bucks past Suns for team's first NBA title since 1971
evidences.content: July 20 (UPI) -- Behind a 50-point performance by superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo, the Milwaukee Bucks beat the Phoenix Suns 105-98 in Game 6 of the NBA Finals on Tuesday night at Fiserv Forum to capture their first championship in a half century. Antetokounmpo, who won the Bill Russell NBA Finals Most Valuable Player Award, became the seventh player in league history to score 50 or more points in a Finals game. The two-time NBA MVP also had 14 rebounds and five blocks in the victory to secure the first title of his young career. [...] Korea Regional Review # Giannis leads Bucks past Suns for team's first NBA title since 1971 Share with X Milwaukee Bucks forward Giannis Antetokounmpo (34) celebrates with the MVP trophy at the conclusion of Game 6 in the 2021 NBA Finals on Tuesday at Fiserv Forum in Milwaukee. Photo by Tannen Maury/EPA-EFE celebrates with the MVP trophy at the conclusion of Game 6 in the 2021 NBA Finals on Tuesday at Fiserv Forum in Milwaukee. evidences.title: 2021 NBA Finals - Wikipedia
evidences.content: The Bucks outscored the Suns 20–0 in the key "Key (basketball)") in the opening period, but Phoenix made eight 3-pointers and trailed just 29–26. With less than five minutes remaining in the first half, the game was tied at 41 before Phoenix ended with a 15–4 run. On their last possession, the Suns made 10 passes, with every team member touching the ball, culminating in Bridges passing the ball to Ayton, who laid it in while being fouled. Phoenix weathered Antetokounmpo's 20-point quarter in the third, when he joined Michael Jordan, Isiah Thomas, and Joe Dumars as the other players to score at least 20 in a quarter during a finals game. Milwaukee cut the lead to six in the fourth quarter, but a 3-pointer by Paul and a basket by Bridges expanded the lead back to double figures, and Booker [...] ### Game 6 [edit] | | | ABC | | | | July 20 9:00pm (8:00pm CDT) | | | | Recap | | | | Phoenix Suns 98, Milwaukee Bucks 105 | | Scoring by quarter: 16–29, 31–13, 30–35, 21–28 | | Pts "Point (basketball)"): Chris Paul 26 Rebs "Rebound (basketball)"): Jae Crowder 13 Asts "Assist (basketball)"): Booker, Paul 5 each | Pts: Giannis Antetokounmpo 50 Rebs: Giannis Antetokounmpo 14 Asts: Jrue Holid...

evidences.title: Suns fall to Bucks in Game 6 of NBA Finals - KOLD
evidences.content: # Suns fall to Bucks in Game 6 of NBA Finals Phoenix Suns guard Devin Booker, right, and Milwaukee Bucks forward Khris Middleton. TUCSON, Ariz. (KOLD News 13) - The Suns will not live to see another day. The Bucks won their first NBA title since 1971. The Suns have never won an NBA crown but were in the Finals for the first time since 1996, when they lost to Michael Jordan’s Chicago Bulls. The Bucks defeated the Suns 98 to 105. The Suns won the opener 118-105 and Game 2 118-108 before losing Game 3 120-100, Game 4 109-103 and Game 5 123-119. ## NBA Finals Schedule Tuesday, July 6 Game 1: Suns 118, Bucks 105 Thursday, July 8 Game 2: Suns 118, Bucks 108 Sunday, July 11 Game 3: Bucks 120, Suns 100 Wednesday, July 14 Game 4: Bucks 109, Suns 103 Saturday, July 17 [...] Game 4: Bucks 109, Suns 103 Saturday, July 17 Game 5: Bucks 123, Suns 119 Tuesday, July 20 Game 6: Suns at Bucks, Bucks lead series 3-2 Thursday, July 22 \Game 7: Bucks at Suns, 6 p.m. (ABC) \ -- if necessary Copyright 2021 KOLD News 13. All rights reserved. Copyright 2021 KOLD News 13. All rights reserved. #### Most Read Two people were shot at Park Place Mall in Tucson on Wednesday, March 18. ###### Fight between tee...

evidences.title: Bucks In 6! Milwaukee Clinches NBA Championship - WPR
evidences.content: Bucks fans cheer in a crowd. Two-time MVP Giannis Antetokounmpo, who played lights-out basketball throughout the finals, carried the team to victory with 50 points,14 rebounds and five blocked shots. It was the third game this series with at least 40 points and 10 rebounds for Antetokounmpo, who became just the seventh player in NBA history to score 50 points or more in a finals game. After the game, NBA Commissioner Adam Silver awarded him the series MVP trophy. In a postgame press conference, Antetokounmpo became emotional talking about his own journey, from poverty in Greece to the NBA championship. Antetokounmpo was born in Greece, but his parents were from Nigeria, and the family was considered stateless for most of his childhood, with few legal rights in the country. [...] # Bucks In 6! Milwaukee Clinches NBA Championship Antetokounmpo's 50-Point Performance Leads Team To First Title Since 1971 Milwaukee Bucks forward Giannis Antetokounmpo with the championship trophy The Milwaukee Bucks are NBA champions for the first time in 50 years. They extinguished the Phoenix Suns 105-98 in Game 6 of the NBA Finals on Tuesday night in front of a hometown crowd of tens of thousands i...

evidences.title: 2021 NBA Finals - Wikipedia
evidences.content: Booker scored 31 points and Paul had 23 as the Suns won 118–108 to take a 2–0 series lead. Phoenix made 20 of its 40 three-pointers, including seven makes from Booker. Bridges added 27 points and seven rebounds for the Suns, and Paul also had eight assists. Antetokounmpo had 42 points and 12 rebounds for Milwaukee. Phoenix was up 65–50 towards the start of the third period, but Antetokounmpo scored 20 points in the quarter, including a stretch of 13 consecutive points for the Bucks, to keep his team in the game. evidences.title: Photos: Relive the Bucks' historic 2021 NBA Finals win
evidences.content: The Bucks beat the Phoenix Suns 105-98 and won four of the best-of-seven games for the championship title July 20, 2021 at Fiserv Forum, and the fans came out to party like it was 1971. About 65,000 fans came to cheer for the Deer that night. Take a look back at our favorite moments. ### - Facebook Twitter # # LATEST NEWS [...] Get the best experience and stay connected to your community with our Spectrum News app. Learn More Get the best experience and stay connected to your community with our Spectrum News app. Learn More Continue in Browser profile icon Get hyperlocal forecasts, radar and weather alerts. Please enter a valid zipcode. Save close region image check check close check close bucks nba championship anniversary 2021 two years later # Photos: 2 years ago, the Bucks became 2-time champs. Relive the Bucks' historic 2021 NBA Finals win MILWAUKEE— Two years ago today, the Milwaukee Bucks became two-time NBA champions.

evidences.title: NBA FINALS 2021 | NBA.com
evidences.content: # NBA FINALS 2021 2021 Finals Game 1: Chris Paul & Devin Booker combine for 59 in opening win #### 2021 Finals Game 1: Chris Paul & Devin Booker combine for 59 in opening win Chris Paul had 32 points and nine assists in an NBA Finals debut that was well worth the wait, Devin Booker scored 27 points and the Suns beat the returning Giannis Antetokounmpo and his Milwaukee Bucks 118-105 in Game 1. 2021 Finals Game 2: Booker, Bridges and Suns' depth show shooting ability #### 2021 Finals Game 2: Booker, Bridges and Suns' depth show shooting ability Devin Booker scored 31 points, Chris Paul had 23 and the Suns beat Milwaukee 118-108 to take a 2-0 series lead. Giannis Antetokounmpo had 42 points and 12 rebounds in his second game back after missing two games. [...] #### 2021 Finals Game 5: Milwaukee steals road game as Holiday, Giannis shine Jrue Holiday's steal and alley-oop pass to Giannis Antetokounmpo for a dunk sealed a wild Game 5 and gave the Bucks a 123-119 victory over the Suns. evidences.title: The Milwaukee Bucks Win the N.B.A. Championship
evidences.content: Share full article 94 Image 1 Giannis Antetokounmpo, center, was named the N.B.A. finals’ most valuable player after scoring 50 points in Game 6. The Bucks finished off the Suns, four games to two.Credit...Jonathan Daniel/Getty Images Image 2: Sopan Deb By Sopan Deb Published July 20, 2021 Updated Oct. 18, 2021 MILWAUKEE — A half-century ago, Kareem Abdul-Jabbar — a young goliath then known as Lew Alcindor — led the Milwaukee Bucks to their first championship. For decades, it was the only time the franchise had reached that height. That is, until now. [...] That is, until now. On Tuesday night, the Bucks capped off their return to greatness. They are once again led by a behemoth with unique skill, this one a 26-year-old player from Greece nicknamed the Greek Freak, Giannis Antetokounmpo. On its home court, Milwaukee defeated the Phoenix Suns, 105-98, in Game 6 of the N.B.A. finals to win its second championship and complete a grueling N.B.A. season of injuries and coronavirus pandemic disruptions. [...] Subscribe to The Times to read as many articles as you like. Sopan Deb is a basketball writer and a contributor to the Culture section. Before joining The Times, he covered Donal...

evidences.title: On this day in 2021, the Milwaukee Bucks won their ...
evidences.content: ## Bucks Nation's Post ### Bucks Nation is at Milwaukee,Wisconsin. July 20, 2025 · Wisconsin · On this day in 2021, the Milwaukee Bucks won their second NBA title in franchise history, defeating the Suns in Game 6 of the Finals and bringing a championship back to the city for the first time in 50 years. Led by Giannis Antetokounmpo ’s unforgettable 50-point performance, the Bucks closed out the series in front of their home crowd. It was a hard-fought, emotional run that showcased the heart and resilience of a team built the right way. With key contributions from Khris Middleton , Jrue Holiday , and the entire roster, Milwaukee powered through the postseason and silenced the doubters... proving that loyalty, grit, and teamwork still win championships. [...] Four years later, that 2021 title run remains one of the most memorable in recent NBA history. 🏆 No photo description available.
